answer:
The maximum coefficient of performance (COP) for a heat pump is given by \( \text{COP} = \frac{T_H}{T_H - T_C} \), with temperatures in Kelvin. Here, \( T_H = 300\,\text{K} \) and \( T_C = 280\,\text{K} \), so \( \text{COP} = \frac{300}{20} = 15 \). The work required is \( W = \frac{Q_H}{\text{COP}} = \frac{15,000}{15} = 1,000\,\text{J} \).  

\boxed{B}
